clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guide clinicians on whether to start with one drug or two drugs for hypertension based on BP stage.
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: At initial diagnosis of hypertension, when deciding on first-line pharmacotherapy.
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Select between initial combination therapy (SPC) or initial monotherapy with titration.

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for HTN management module.
Classify BP as Stage 1 or Stage 2 based on SBP/DBP.
Present the specific recommendation based on stage.
Select appropriate first-line agents (ACEi, ARB, CCB, Thiazide).
If Stage 2, recommend SPC; check for contraindications.
Check for dual RAS blockade (ACEi+ARB) to flag harm.
Schedule follow-up for titration or BP check.

rules: If SBP >= 140 AND DBP >= 90 (Stage 2): Apply COR 1 (LOE B-R) -> Recommend 2 agents (SPC).
If SBP 130-139 AND DBP 80-89 (Stage 1): Apply COR 2a (LOE C-EO) -> Recommend 1 agent initially.
If user tries to combine ACEi + ARB + Renin Inhibitor: Trigger COR 3: Harm (LOE A) Alert.

categories.name: Stage 2 Hypertension - Initial Combination Therapy
categories.action: Initiate 2 first-line agents of different classes, ideally SPC

categories.name: Stage 1 Hypertension - Initial Monotherapy
categories.reasoning: Reasonable to start with one, then titrate/add as needed

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: For Stage 1: Start low-dose monotherapy. For Stage 2: Start SPC.
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Follow up in 2-4 weeks to check BP control and potassium/creatinine if on RAS blockers.
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm BP control at follow up; titrate if needed.
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if ACEi+ARB combination is ordered (COR 3: Harm).
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log initial therapy choice (Monotherapy vs SPC) and COR/LOE assignment.
